A narrow passage west of here leads through dark briars and opens to this small
glade. Tall trees from a dense copse of pines to the east look over this area.
In the centre of the glade, six name-stones are arranged in a circle, facing
outward.
name: Shadowy Glade

desc:
Several grassy, green mounds rise from the brushy field blocking travel to the
north and west. Scattered about, both on and around the barrows, are
name-stones. Most have toppled over and are weathered to illegibility. A break
in a rusted iron fence to the south leads to open plains. To the east, the
burial grounds continue.
name: Field of Barrows

desc:
This ridge overlooks a deep, wooded ravine to the south. North is a large brushy
field filled with mounds, partially enclosed by a long fence. A large section of
the fence has broken away from its stone pillars, offering easy passage. West
and east of here a gentle slope leads down to grassy plains.
name: Edge of a Ravine

desc:
Long, slender blades of grass lie atop one another in all directions, as the
wind has never been able to figure out from which direction it should blow. A
deep wooded ravine cuts across the plains to the south, terminating near a dense
copse of pines to the east. To the north, just as the land slopes upward, a
fence of rusted iron and stone pillars blocks passage.
name: Grassy Plains

desc:
A deep, wooded ravine cuts across the plains south of here, travelling east as
far as one can see. A rusted iron fence blocks passage to the north, beyond
which can be seen prairie filled with green earthen mounds and large grey
stones.
name: Near a Ravine

desc:
This tomb is recessed six feet into the ground. A short stairway, partially
covered by earth and grass, grants an unhindered exit. The tomb is built of fine
stonework, with even the floor being crafted of bricks unmarked by any cracks or
gaps. Six stone coffins adorn the walls.
name: Dry, Clean Tomb

desc:
Small rock cairns occasionally mark the remains of a stone path travelling up
and down through the hills. To the west, it descends a gentle slope to the
valley floor below. The the south, it travels on a narrow knife edge atop a hill
towards a wide, flat plateau. A cool, shaded trough can be traversed downward
along the eastern slope.
name: Broken Path through Some Hills

desc:
This is a wide, flat plateau high above the surrounding lands. It was once
dominated by a sizeable manor, but it has largely collapsed, with only two walls
remaining. A central room of the manor is exposed to the sky, within which a
tall chair carved of stone but made to look like wood stands untouched by time.
name: Ruined Manor on a Flat Plateau

desc:
A deep V-shaped trough is cut naturally into the eastern slope of a hill, and
travels upwards and downwards. Below here, the trough is heavily shaded by trees
and covered by brush. It appears to drop below the ground, but it is impossible
to see where it leads.
name: Trough in Some Hills

desc:
This is the western end of a sprawling valley that winds its way between rolling
hills. The valley floor is covered in tall, golden grass that rustles gently in
the breeze. In the distance, a dry streambed crosses the valley. To the west,
the hills rise up gently, their peaks just visible above the grass. To the east,
the valley continues as far as the eye can see.
name: Western End of a Valley

desc:
Tall grass populates the heart of a valley. Hills rise up north and south of
here, while the soft valley floor stretches east and west. Large, shady trees
spot the landscape. The grass is matted down here and there, signs that large
animals bed down here often.
name: Heart of a Valley

desc:
This is the eastern end of a valley, surrounded by towering hills on all sides
except where it stretches outward, leading towards the west. The ground is
covered in tall, golden grass that rustles gently in the breeze. Gentle slopes
lead up into the hills north of here.
name: Eastern End of a Valley

desc:
This is the crest of a steep, green hill. The rolling terrain is dotted with
large rocks, jagged outcroppings, and twisted shrubs. The grass is tall and
swaying, rustling with every breeze. The air is filled with a strong, fermented
scent from bear scat. The area is alive with other signs of bear activity, such
as footprints and scratch marks on tree trunks.
name: Rolling Hills

desc:
The slopes of the hills in the area are covered by fallen trees. A very strong
wind must have passed through here at one time, uprooting many trees while
snapping others at the trunk. Even while lying on their side, many trees
continue to bud and grow leaves through the seasons. Plains open up south and
west of here, while to the north there is an expanse of more hills.
name: Fallen Trees

desc:
A rolling, verdant landscape of steep hills stretches out in all directions. The
ground is dotted with rocks and boulders, some as tall as trees, and the air is
thick with the buzz of insects and the chirping of birds. Soft grasses cover the
ground, providing an easy surface to travel over.
name: Verdant Hills

desc:
The floor of this small valley is dotted with blackened trees and singed
undergrowth. A jagged scar runs down the hillside north and west of here,
marking the path of a great forest fire. The scent of charred wood and
vegetation hangs faintly in the air.
name: Burnt Valley

desc:
Plains travel westward through a narrow valley. In all other directions, hills
rise up to block or hinder travel. In the nearly vertical face of a hill to the
north, a wide fissure seems to lead deep into the hill. In front of it is a
small stone circle with a charred interior, the remains of a long dormant
campfire.
name: Before a Wide Fissure

desc:
The grass on this plain grows wild and verdant, reaching well above four feet
high. Patches of wheat sprout here and there, never cultivated, but rather,
growing wildly. The soil is loose, and dozens of small holes and burrows are
visible within, home of many mice and snakes.
name: Wild Grasses

desc:
A tall, lonely hill, separated from the region of hills to the south, stands
sentinel over the bleak plains. Branches and twigs litter the ground. Open
plains surrounded by hills in the distance can be seen in all directions save
south.
name: Sentinel Hill

desc:
A high plain stretches between the surrounding hills and peaks. Scrub and brush
are all that grow here. The ground is rocky, dry and cracked, seeming to retain
very little moisture. The terrain is much too harsh to travel any direction but
east.
name: High, Rocky Plains

desc:
Only thistles grow more than a few inches high here. Most of the grass is shorn
close to the ground, looking to be frequently grazed on. The earth is lumpy and
recently churned in parts, showing evidence of many hooved animals travelling
through this area.
name: Trodden Plains

desc:
Grasses grow lush and thick here, their broad blades a dark green. The ground is
soft and spongy from an abundance of moisture. Water runs off the surrounding
hills and saturates in the earth here in this valley. The valley floor can be
followed west and south from here.
name: Damp Plains

desc:
Rainfall running off the surrounding hills collects here, filling stangant pools
of water. Swarms of midges thrive here, and their humming fills the air
constantly. Grass and other groundcover grows only sporadically, leaving much of
the terrain soft and muddy.
name: Soggy Plains

desc:
A narrow valley passes northward between the hills here. The ground is grassy
and easily traversed, but becomes more wild and overgrown as one travels west.
Tiny flowers can be seen peeking above the undergrowth here and there. Grey
stones litter the valley floor beneath a smattering of oak trees.
name: Narrow Valley

desc:
Thistles and spiny bushes growing over four feet high fill this area. The air
and vegetation are filled with all manner of stinging and biting insects. A
constant buzzing drowns out all other sounds. The ground is soft and muddy in
parts, with thousands of pock-marks where insects have gathered balls of mud for
their nests.
name: Buzzing Brush

desc:
An enormous tree with thick, interwoven branches towers over the thistles and
bushes. The trunk rises only a few feet above the ground before splitting into
several large branches and hundreds of smaller ones. Its centre is filled by a
massive hornet nest made of wood pulp and mud, nearly the size of a small
cottage. A loud buzzing fills the air.
name: Massive Hornet Nest

desc:
Tree covered hills rise and fall around this region. The forest is old and
mature, with rough, wide trunks that stretch more than eighty feet in the air.
The forest floor is covered with tracks of deer, foxes, and rabbits, and the
branches above are filled with nests.
name: Wooded Hills

desc:
A tree covered ridge travels along the top of a series of hills. Its slopes are
too steep to traverse down to the north and south, leaving east and west the
only directions of passage. A gentle decline to the west leads deeper into the
forest.
name: Woody Ridge

desc:
A steep, rocky slope angles sharply out of the forest west and south of here,
casting this area in shadows every day starting at mid afternoon. A more gentle
slope rises in the opposite direction. Wildlife thrives here, both flora and
fauna. There is no clear trail through the woods, but the forest floor is clear
enough to offer meandering passage to the north and east.
name: Forest against a Rocky Slope

desc:
A steep escarpment west of here, along with tall, full trees, keeps the forest
cool and shaded during most of the day. At night, the canopy hides the stars
above. Animals seem to flourish here, both on the ground and in the trees. On
the occasion when there is a breeze from the east, it brings with it a dank,
musty odour.
name: Dense Forest

desc:
Deep in this forest, trees lean inward and the ground suddenly slopes downward
in a conical fashion. At the bottom of this depression is a gaping sinkhole,
about eight feet in diameter, that drops deep into the earth. Large, grey
mushrooms with rounded caps grow around its perimeter. A musty odour fills the
air, coming from within.
name: Above a Sinkhole

desc:
A glade opens up in the forest, surrounding a large pond of fresh, clear water.
Cattails grow along its banks. Game trails lead in and out of the forest,
creating several paths into the water. To the north and west the forest opens up
to brushy plains, while to the south the trees grow taller.
name: Shimmering Pond

desc:
Many trees grow in this area, but their growth has been stunted, and none stand
more than four feet tall. Their trunks are never more than a couple inches wide,
and their thin branches droop to the ground. East of here, the trees stand
taller and form a proper forest. To the south and west, rocky hills rise up.
name: Stunted Brushland

desc:
Tall, steep hills rise and fall in this area. The tops of many are bald and
lumpy, with great mounds of rock rising above the earth and grass. A narrow
ridge can be traversed to the south, leading deeper and higher into the hills. A
rocky slope to the east heads down towards a forest.
name: Lumpy Ridge

desc:
Large, grey rocks rise all around here, at the peak of a long hill. Narrow gaps
between the boulders offer passage towards the north and east. Wind occasionally
whistles between the rocks, but otherwise little can be heard.
name: Narrow Passage between Some Boulders

desc:
Boulders and shards of rock have broken away from surrounding hills to create an
uneven, rocky slope leading to the south. In that direction can be seen the flat
summit of a tall hill. Steep slopes drop off to the north and east, offering a
view of a forest hundreds of feet below.
name: Talus Slope

desc:
A flat plateau stretches out here, upon one of the tallest hills in the region.
A steep slope drops off dramatically to the south, offering a commanding view of
the lands below. They are mostly open plains, but far to the southwest can be
seen a tall, stone structure built into a hillside. To the west, the plateau
continues, while a rocky talus to the north offers passage down.
name: High Plateau

desc:
Mounds of rock rise steeply to the west and north, and a slope drops
dramatically to the south, leaving only a narrow passage on which to stand. The
rocky crags are filled with birds nests, some of them quite large, as if woven
from tree branches instead of twigs. Over the cliff to the south can be seen
open plains below, as well as a tall, stone structure built into a hillside far
to the southwest. The plateau widens to the east.
name: Narrow Plateau filled with Nests

desc:
Thick brush carpets the area. Small, thorny vines growing low on the ground
share space with willowy ferns. Limbs from woody bushes snarl into nearly
impenetrable masses. The north and west become impassable due to the growth of
thick vegetation.
name: Isolated Brush

desc:
A mixture of soft mosses and grasses cover the ground except for sporadic hoof
and paw prints that sank through to dirt. The spongy ground is kept wet by the
dense vegetation. Bushes as tall as a horse curl around each other in a wild
tangle. The brush becomes impenetrable to the north.
name: Wild Brush

desc:
The eastern section of this area is covered with a multitude of tangled weeds
and vines. The different plants appear to have grown together over time into an
impenetrable wall of sinewy shrubs. The rest of the area is filled with tough
roots curling in and out of the soil and leafy weeds.
name: Tremendous Tangle

desc:
Thick, thorny brush covers the floor of this deciduous forest, making it
challenging to explore very far. High in the branches can be seen dozens of
papery hornet nests. A soft, continual buzzing in the air makes it clear they
are inhabited. To the east, the trees mostly fade away, but the brush continues.
name: Brush Filled Forest

desc:
Snaking green vines flourish here with some stems as thick as small trees. The
plants coil and twist among each other, preventing movement to the north and
west. Some of the vines towards the east appear to have dried and become
brittle.
name: Crowded Vines

desc:
Plants and bushes are impressively thick here. It is impossible to move without
leaves and spindly branches impeding progress over the land. There are only a
few small routes visible to traverse east and west. The ground slopes down
dramatically to the east and it may be too challenging to return from that
direction.
name: Narrow Thicket

desc:
A large gully dominates the area. A cascade of bushes descend from the west over
a sharp edge that is impossible to climb. Some hardy roots live in the shallow
mud at the lowest part of the land. Sparse shrubs and patches of grass appear on
several paths out of the gully to the east and south.
name: Wide Gully

desc:
An imposing canyon forms to the north while the sides open up to the east and
west. Lumpy hills covered in thick grasses cover the area. Taller bluffs block
movement south and can be seen further east. Scattered boulders grow moss in a
variety of colours.
name: Canyon Opening

desc:
From the gully to the north the land slopes up towards the south before meeting
a set of large boulders. The large stones corral inhabitants away from the south
and west. Smaller boulders spaced erratically make movement tedious and block
longer views.
name: Rising Hill

desc:
The ground undulates on a large scale like an ocean wave frozen in time. The
dirt is unusually dark. Patches of stiff grass and weeds grow in small mounds on
the black hills. To the east, a chasm reaches down to darkness. Its jagged edges
snake around to the north and south allowing movement only to the west.
name: Dark Hills

desc:
Tall canyon walls slowly curve allowing paths to the east and south. Several
animal tracks have cleared narrow paths through the tall grass. The canyon walls
slope slowly as they meet the floor of the valley. The walls are too steep to
hold any vegetation except an occasional tough bush.
name: Curving Canyon

desc:
Bulbous rocks hang off the sides of the canyon to the north and south. The
boulders precariously rest appearing as if the slightest movement could unleash
them down the sides of the canyon into the valley below. The valley is not
smooth, with rough hills where the ground has piled around stones that fell from
the cliffs long ago.
name: Rocky Canyon

desc:
A tall rock wall stands to the south as a last connection to the canyon to the
west. Hills sprawl to the north and east. Large rocks have broken off from the
wall to the south and have tumbled down and rest, growing fungus and moss. Some
animal tracks are visible as they converge into the canyon.
name: Canyon Mouth

desc:
Stumps from long broken trees and low brush sporadically cover the landscape.
The hills show long striations from top to bottom as if raked by a giant claw.
Inside the long marks can be seen dirt of deep brown, red and tan. Patches of
sand are exposed near the bottom of the hills.
name: Patchy Hills

desc:
Tall rock formations block all movement except back to the north. Trenches in
the ground make traversing the terrain difficult. Shallow mud congeals in the
bottom of the deep furrows. Grass and weeds sparsely habitate the ground outside
the troughs.
name: Dead End

desc:
These grassy plains roll up and down in verdant waves. Flat boulders poke a few
inches out above the soil, their grey surfaces dotted green and brown with
lichen. Gentle slopes lead to the south and east, while tall and impassable
hills block travel elsewhere.
name: Rolling Green Plains

desc:
The hills in this area are rugged and bare, with only the occasional grasses and
shrubs growing on them. The soil is rocky, loose, and challenging to travel
over. The only signs of wildlife are of small mammals and birds that can easily
shelter in nooks in the rocks.
name: Bleak Hills

desc:
Rugged hills rolls out in every direction, hindering visibility and travel.
There is no flat path to walk on, and the soil along the sides of the hills is
gravelly and loose in spots. An occasional breeze passes through the hills,
carrying with it the scent of pine or sage.
name: Rugged Hills

desc:
Towering hills rise and fall in every direction. They are rocky and steep-sided,
with extensive areas of exposed rock and little clinging vegetation. A few claw
marks and abandoned nests left in nooks and cracks attest to the presence of at
least some wildlife here.
name: Rocky Hills

desc:
This nook is located below steep slopes in all directions except the east. It is
well protected from the wind and elements, allowing heavy brush and short, wide
trees to grow with abandon. Along with the growth here, tracks of rabbits and
foxes can be seen in the passages through the brush and grass.
name: Nook in Some Hills

desc:
A chasm looms deep to the west. Some trees grow on the edge of the precipice
curving west and then upwards as if they were almost pulled into the depths when
the ground broke. The area outside the chasm is scarred with jagged holes and
uneven terrain.
name: Sloping Hills

desc:
There are several huge boulders arranged in a large circle of flat ground. The
great stones are covered in a thick moss and seem to have been stationary for a
very long time. Inside the circle are very faint imprints in the dirt, partially
covered in short grass. Outside the circle are rising hills.
name: Circle of Stones

desc:
The ground rises above the surrounding hills. The view from the highest point
reveals expansive undulating knolls in all directions. The view is interrupted
only by a few trees managing to survive on this exposed piece of land. A steep
drop off from the high ground allows movement only to the south.
name: Tall Hill

desc:
The terrain is difficult with large stones wrapped with prickly bushes and stiff
vines. Any path is forced to wind around the green-wrapped boulders. The land
slopes up at a tall angle towards the north leaving the only safe option for
movement back to the south.
name: Rising Brush

desc:
A tear-shaped cavern is here beneath the earth. It narrows to a point to the
north, where a slim tunnel heads in that direction. From there, walls of earth
and rock curve around to create a room nearly twenty feet in diameter and eight
feet tall near its centre. The floor slants towards the east. It is covered in
rocks and loose soil, looking to have been recently disturbed in places.
name: Rocky Cavern

desc:
A scrubby grassland stretches south, a vast sea of waving green grass. Despite
its seemingly endless expanse, there is a sense of unease in the air. A huge
wall of thorns blocks passage to the north, the sharp spines and twisted vines a
warning to those who would venture too close. Thorny vines and roots creep in
from the north, snaking their way through the grass and tangling around anything
in their path. The air is thick with the smell of dust and the sound of the wind
whistling through the grass.
name: Scrubby Grassland

desc:
A huge wall of thorns curves to the north and east, and the ground immediately
bordering it is bare, the earth baked hard by the sun and devoid of any
vegetation. Yet beyond that, the grassland stretches out to the south, an
endless sea of green waves. Despite its harsh appearance, the thorny grassland
is home to a variety of hardy creatures that have adapted to the unforgiving
environment. These include snakes, lizards, and rodents, as well as the
occasional wandering traveller seeking a passage through this treacherous
region.
name: Thorny Grassland

desc:
This is a quiet field, with the exception of the sounds of birds and a gentle
breeze rustling through the grass. Yellow and orange wildflowers cover the
landscape, growing wherever they can with no prejudice. Animal tracks and paths
crisscross across the terrain.
name: Quiet Field

desc:
To the north of the field is a massive wall of woven vines, covered in sharp
thorns. The vines and roots of the wall creep southward, spreading out across
the ground and snaking toward the field. The earth at the base of the wall is
barren and lifeless, stripped of vegetation. Despite this, the field itself is a
verdant expanse, the grass tall and lush.
name: Field along a Thistle Patch

desc:
To the north of the field stands a massive wall of vines, blocking access to the
thistle patch beyond. The vines are thick and woven together, covered in sharp
thorns that deter would-be intruders. The earth at the base of the wall is
barren and lifeless, stripped of grass and vegetation. Despite this, the field
itself is a rolling expanse of green, the grass tall and lush.
name: Field South of a Thistle Patch

desc:
The field lies to the south of a huge patch of thistle, the spiky plants
standing tall and proud. There is a huge wall separating the field from the
thistle patch, and it is covered in sharp thorns. The field itself is a rolling
expanse of green, the grass tall and lush.
name: Field South of a Thistle Patch

desc:
A quiet, tranquil sea of green grass spreads out in every direction,
disappearing into the horizons. Yellow and orange wildflowers dot the landscape
with bees and other insects buzzing about them. Small paths have been worn into
the ground by animals that traverse them.
name: Quiet Field

desc:
A wall of thick, green vines, covered in long, sharp thorns, blocks passage to
the north. It rises suddenly from the otherwise flat grassland bordering it to
the south, and the view beyond it is dark and ominous. In the south and east,
the ground is covered in a lush, green blanket of grass.
name: Grassy Plain

desc:
Thorny thistles dominate the landscape, their spiky leaves a constant threat.
The dense vegetation grows thinner towards the ground, allowing for the
occasional glimpse of animal paths and tracks. It is a wild and untamed place,
one that humbles even the bravest of travellers. The air is still and quiet, the
only sound the rustling of the thistles as some unseen creature passes by.
name: Thorny Thistles

desc:
A large, wooden wagon rests on four giant wheels. There are trails cut into the
ground of the barn beneath them. The floor is dusty, and the walls are thin. The
planks were sloppily secured to the walls, with large cracks between each one,
allowing for a view beyond them.
name: Dusty Barn

desc:
The rolling plains are covered in green grass that appears to go on for an
indefinite distance. The gentle breeze carries with it the sounds of rodents
scurrying through the underbrush and insects buzzing about their business. Low
hills rise and fall in the distance, offering a hint of the adventures that
await those who venture forth. The air is fresh and clean.
name: Rolling Plains

desc:
The vast grassland stretches out, a flat and seemingly endless expanse of waving
green grass. The earth beneath is fertile and rich, nurtured by the warm sun and
gentle rains. The air is filled with the sound of birds singing and the rustle
of grass in the breeze.
name: Vast, Rolling Grassland

desc:
Spiky thistles fill the air, their dense foliage a constant obstacle. Animal
tracks and paths can be seen amongst the dense, thorny vegetation, evidence of
the diverse creatures that call this place home. The ground is barren, covered
in a blanket of dead leaves, adding to the sense of desolation in this wild and
untamed place. It is a harsh and unforgiving environment, one that requires
careful footing. Despite the challenges it presents, it is a place of great
beauty, with a sense of ruggedness that cannot be denied.
name: Dense Thistles

desc:
Almost all of the vegetation is covered in thorns, making it difficult to move
through the dense underbrush. Vines hang low, tangling around ankles and
tripping up those who are not careful. Roots snake across the ground, hidden
beneath the thick foliage. Thistles are everywhere, their spiky leaves a
constant threat. It is a wild and untamed place, one that demands respect and
caution from all who enter.
name: Thorny Thistles

desc:
The blue thistles seem to glow with an otherworldly light, and the rustling of
the brambles takes on a haunting melody. The thistle patch presents a prickly
embrace of thorny brambles to those who approach it. A closer examination of the
thistle patch reveals an intricate network of vines and brambles that function
to hold it all together. The ground beneath the thistle patch is covered in a
thick layer of soft, damp earth.
name: Deep inside a Thistle Patch

desc:
Deep in the thistle canopy lies a woodland clearing, where the air is filled
with the scent of pine and wildflowers. The blue thistles are poking through the
tangle of brambles, growing even taller, their spines reaching up to the sky
like spears.
name: Clearing in the Thistle Patch

desc:
Nestled between the rolling hills and misty forests, lies a vast expanse of blue
thistle. For two miles in every direction, the land is covered in a sea of these
enchanting flowers, with their spiny stems reaching up to the sky and their
delicate blooms swaying in the breeze. The blue thistle leaves are a shimmering
blue-grey, and their flowers are like delicate snowflakes, each one containing a
small steel-blue, white, or rose-colour blossom with a cone centre.
name: Thistle Patch

desc:
The thistles, tall and proud, stand in a regal display of blue-purple hues. This
thistle patch is mixed with thorny weeds with jagged leaves and small white
flowers that add to the overall beauty of the patch. Thorny bushes provide a
natural barrier around the perimeter of the patch. The bushes are tall and
spindly, with sharp thorns protruding from their branches in all directions.
name: Thistles

desc:
The dense underbrush is covered in thorns and spines, making it difficult to
navigate. Thick roots blanket the ground, tripping up those who are not careful.
Creeping thistle weeds with blue heads add to the treacherous terrain, their
spiky leaves a constant threat. It is a dense and unforgiving place, one that
requires careful footing and a brave heart.
name: Dense, Thorn-covered Thistles

desc:
The dense, dry brush seems to go on forever, thick thistles poking out at every
turn. Animal tracks and paths crisscross the dry, grassy ground, evidence of the
many creatures that call this place home. It is a wild and untamed place, with a
certain rugged beauty that draws the eye.
name: Dense, Thorn-covered Thickets

desc:
The branches in this thorny orchard are gnarled and twisted like the ancient
roots of the earth. The foliage has moved high up towards the sun, leaving lower
branches without leaves. Amidst the trees in the orchard stands a strange arch,
its ancient stones weathered by time and forgotten by history. The brush
surrounding the arch is thick and tangled, almost as if it were alive. A
slightly musty smell surrounds the damp darkness in the heart of the arch.
name: Thistle Patch

desc:
Amidst the thistles, bracken grows in abundance, creating a lush and vibrant
undergrowth. The scent of the thistles and the bracken is strong and sweet,
filling the air with a heady perfume. At the heart of the thistle patch, there
is a limestone outcrop, rising from the ground like a great natural sculpture.
In the scrubland around the thistles, sparse vegetation struggles to survive,
with dead oak leaves littering the ground in abundance.
name: Thistle Patch

desc:
The rim of the plateau offers breathtaking views of the surrounding landscape,
miles in every direction. Large stands of tall, thick grasses cover many of the
lower slopes. The rocky terrain can be treacherous for the unwary traveller, as
hidden beneath the thick growth of grass and foliage lie hidden crevices and
sinkholes.
name: Rim of a Plateau

desc:
A mixture of stale air and the scent of wild animal gives this tunnel a musty
odour. Every shade of brown is represented in the clay and earth that makes up
the walls. The natural beauty is only broken by the fine strands of root hanging
down from above like hair from a curly, shaggy dog.
name: Musty Tunnel

desc:
The blue thistle patch stretches as far as the eye can see. Thorny vines with
pale prickly stems seem to wrap themselves around anything in their path.
Despite its rough exterior, the thistle patch is teeming with life. Snails and
slugs are drawn to the thistles, feasting on their leaves and using their spiny
stems for protection. Deeper into the landscape, the scrubland becomes more
rugged and rocky.
name: Thistle Patch

desc:
The thistles themselves are a deep shade of blue, their spiky stems jutting out
in all directions, daring any unwary traveller to approach too closely. The
patch stretches out as far as the eye can see, a sea of prickly blue. The
scrubland surrounding the thistle patch is treacherous, full of dense thickets
overgrown with vines. The scent of thistles in the air becomes denser when
approaching the thistle patch. It is a powerful combination of sweetness and
sharpness that invades the senses.
name: Tangled Thistle Patch in the Scrubland

desc:
The thistle patch is abundant with a profusion of tall, spiny thistles, standing
proud and majestic in their deep indigo hue, like a sea of blue fire that dances
in the breeze. Pearl-bordered fritillaries with their distinctive black chevrons
on the edge of their wings flutter around the patch, seemingly enchanted by the
vibrant blue blooms. The gorse shrubs, with their prickly thorns and fragrant
yellow blossoms, provide a contrasting yellow accent to the sea of blue
thistles. The sharp fragrance of the gorse shrubs intermingles with the sweet
scent of the thistles, creating a unique aroma that is both heady and
invigorating.
name: Thistles and Gorse Shrubs

desc:
Inside the dune slack, the blue thistles grow in abundance despite the harsh
winds and shifting sands. Here, the thistle flowers are smaller but no less
vibrant, their colours standing out against the pale landscape. A babbling brook
and a forgotten ruin are hidden among the thistles, a reminder of the long
history of this land. Birds nest among the thistles, using their sharp spines as
a natural defence against predators.
name: Thistle-covered Dune Slack

desc:
The thistles are tall and majestic, towering over the other flora in the area.
Their vivid blue hue is so intense that it appears almost luminescent. The
thistles are not to be approached lightly, for they are covered in sharp thorns
that are capable of inflicting great pain. The thorns are arranged in a
spiralling pattern along the stems.
name: Thistle Patch

desc:
The dense brush stretches out all around, a tangle of twisted branches and sharp
thorns. Blue thistle flowers peek out from among the gnarled branches, with
spiky petals. The ground beneath the brush is hard and rocky, making it
difficult to navigate through the dense undergrowth. Despite the beauty of the
thistle flowers, there is an undeniable feeling of danger lurking in this place.
name: Dense Thistles

desc:
The brush is dense and impenetrable, stretching out in all directions as far as
the eye can see. The tangled branches and sharp thorns make it difficult to
navigate through the undergrowth. The ground beneath the brush is hard and
rocky, making it difficult to move through the dense tangle of branches. Despite
the beauty of the thistle flowers, there is an air of danger lurking in this
place, a sense that something unseen is waiting just beyond the reach of the
brush.
name: Dense Thistles

desc:
The underbrush is a dense and treacherous landscape, where thick roots twist and
tangle beneath the surface. A riot of thorns and thistles sprout from the earth,
their spiky leaves and stems reaching up toward the sky. The air is heavy with
the scent of verdant growth, and the rustling of leaves is the only sound that
breaks the silence. It is a place that beckons the adventurer, but also one that
requires caution and attentiveness.
name: Patch of Thistles

desc:
The landscape here is dominated by a thicket of thorns and spines, a formidable
barrier to those attempting to pass through. The ground is covered in a tangle
of roots and vines, making it difficult to find solid footing. Animal paths wind
through the underbrush, but they are narrow and perilous, often overgrown and
hard to follow. Blue thistle flowers are abundant, their spiky petals a constant
threat to those who stray too far from the path.
name: Patch of Thistles

desc:
The underbrush is a tangled mass of thorns and vines, with thick roots snaking
their way through the earth. The air is heavy with the scent of green, growing
things, and the rustle of leaves is the only sound to be heard. The ground
underfoot is uneven, treacherous with twisted roots and hidden thorns waiting to
catch the unwary. It is a place of danger, but also of beauty, as the vines
twist and curl in a riot of greenery. It is a place that begs to be explored,
but also one that demands caution and care.
name: Thick Patch of Thistles

desc:
The dense brush is thick with roots and vines covered in sharp thorns and
spines, a formidable obstacle to navigate. Animal paths provide some respite,
but even they are treacherous, winding through the underbrush with abandon. The
ground beneath is hard and barren, with only the occasional blue thistle flower
providing a splash of colour.
name: Thick Thistles

desc:
Animal paths and trails wind their way through the dense growth, providing a
hint of civilisation in this wild and untamed place. The sound of rustling
leaves fills the air, as the plants and flowers move in the breeze. Amidst the
green foliage, the vivid blue of thistle flowers can be seen, their spiky petals
adding a splash of colour to the otherwise monochromatic landscape.
name: Patch of Dense Thistles

desc:
Amid a wild, untamed field lies a large thistle patch, with thick flowery
brambles and overgrown grass swaying in the wind. The thistles rise above the
brambles, creating a maze-like canopy. The rich, blue thistles are towering and
impressive, with sharp, pointed leaves that seem to reach for the sky. The air
is filled with the scent of lichen.
name: Thick Flowery Brambles

desc:
Amidst the blue thistles, there are stinging nettles that seem to compete with
the thistles for dominance. The surrounding foliage is dense and varied. The
nettles have a pale green colour and tiny hairs that can cause a painful sting
to anyone who dares to touch them. Caution should be exercised when entering the
thistle patch since the thorns and nettles within it may prick or sting.
name: Patch of Thistles and Nettles

desc:
The blue thistles grow in clusters amid the heather and bracken in the
moorlands. The land is wild and untamed, the perfect home for these hardy and
resilient plants. The thistles themselves are remarkably resilient, able to
survive in even the harshest conditions. The blue thistles are not the only
inhabitants of this patch of wilderness. Also, a few solitary trees can be seen
in the distance.
name: Thistle Patch in the Moorlands

desc:
Long gashes mar ever inch of the earthen walls of this tunnel, evidently gouged
by some long, sharp claws. A musty stench of fur permeates the space, giving the
impression that something must live here. The only way out seems to be to the
west.
name: End of a Musty Tunnel

desc:
The grass here is thick and lush, a vibrant shade of green that seems to go on
forever. Wildflowers dot the field, adding splashes of colour to the verdant
expanse. The breeze rustles the grass and carries with it the soft buzz of
insects, creating a sense of peace and serenity.
name: Rolling Grassland

desc:
The field is a rolling expanse of green, the grass tall and luscious. At its
border stands a patch of thistle, the spiky plants rising proudly against the
sky. The thistle's blue flowers add a touch of colour to the landscape, their
petals attracting a variety of pollinators.
name: Field Bordering a Thistle Patch
